title: NTT/FFT bignum multiplication backend (2nd independent backend); throughput up, determinism preserved
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. A clean-room NTT/FFT multiplication backend lands as a second, genuinely independent bignum path; independence must be preserved because it is what the verification gate relies on, and the change must not break bit-reproducibility. It depends on core-002 and ci-006.
objective: Implement a clean-room NTT/FFT bignum multiplication backend as a second independent path that improves throughput while preserving determinism and verification independence.
acceptanceCriteria: A clean-room NTT/FFT multiplication backend lands as a second, genuinely independent bignum path (independence preserved for verification).
Documented >= Xx throughput improvement on a fixed benchmark; passes the determinism + verification tests - the PR does not merge if it breaks bit-reproducibility.
output: A PR adding the clean-room NTT/FFT bignum backend as a second independent path with preserved determinism.
